stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM release.
It is tracked on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated pipeline is text-generation, confirming its primary application as a generative AI model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core areas of transformers and text-generation within the AI/ML ecosystem.
The mention of Meta's Llama family indicates continued relevance in large language model development.

stories.title: Jeff Bezos’ AI startup aims to build an ‘artificial general engineer’
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Jeff Bezos has launched a new AI startup named Prometheus.
The startup's goal is to create an 'artificial general engineer' using AI.
Prometheus will focus on developing AI tools to assist in the design of physical products.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability was found in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows unauthorized access, leading to the theft of gigabytes of sensitive data from affected organizations.
The vulnerability is described as being among the most critical in the software category.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat against Russian soldiers.
The deployment signals a significant integration of AI modules onto military drones and robots.
The event highlights a rapid advancement in the practical application of autonomous systems in modern warfare.

stories.title: Google sues Chinese cybercrime network that used Gemini to automate scams
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is taking legal action against a Chinese cybercrime network.
The criminal operation utilized Gemini (Google's AI) to automate fraudulent scams.
The scams reportedly targeted hundreds of thousands of individuals.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The core argument is that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent environment (resources, constraints, interfaces).
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, sometimes surpassing human-designed methods.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'EurekAgent,' an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
The core argument is that the bottleneck for autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment.
These agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions, sometimes outperforming human-designed methods.

stories.title: CRISPR tech selectively shreds cancer cells, including "undruggable" cancers
stories.author: gmays
bullet_points: CRISPR technology has shown promise in selectively targeting and destroying cancer cells.
The research specifically addresses 'undruggable' cancers, indicating a major advancement in oncology.
The news originates from Hacker News, suggesting it is a topic of high interest within the tech/science community.

stories.title: Twenty One Zero-Days in FFmpeg
stories.author: redbell
bullet_points: Discovery of twenty-one zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg.
FFmpeg is a foundational, widely used multimedia framework, making these vulnerabilities highly critical.
This represents a significant security risk requiring immediate attention from developers and users.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML topics like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The context suggests tracking the performance and trends of large language models.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance boost translates to higher quality model outputs.
Users benefit from faster response times and reduced memory consumption.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for detecting compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method tests if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived by composing a provided decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by leveraging operad theory, a formalism for iterated substitution.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion across model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
The research identifies a pattern where production coding agents are adopting 'Recursive Agent Harnesses' instead of simple model calls.
These harnesses involve full agents equipped with filesystem tools, code execution, and planning, exemplified by recent work at Anthropic.

stories.title: Malware developers added nuclear and biological weapons text to to their spyware
stories.author: marc__1
bullet_points: Malware developers have incorporated references to nuclear and biological weapons into their spyware.
The threat actors are specifically targeting developers in the bioinformatics and MCP sectors.
The news highlights the increasing sophistication and alarming nature of modern cyber threats.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
A key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
The framework includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM usage.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, meaning processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI capabilities for coding assistance.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Elon Musk is the world’s first trillionaire
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Elon Musk has reached a net worth of one trillion dollars.
This milestone was achieved following the IPO of SpaceX (SPCX).
His wealth is derived from his holdings in SpaceX and other companies like Tesla.

stories.title: SpaceX’s massive IPO: all the latest news
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly conducting a massive Initial Public Offering (IPO).
The IPO is described as encompassing SpaceX's rocket, AI, and social media ventures.
The financial event is noted as contributing to Elon Musk achieving a trillionaire status.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX's transition to a publicly valued entity signals increased investor scrutiny on profitability.
The article focuses on the future trajectory of SpaceX, specifically in relation to its AI capabilities.
The core theme is the pressure on a major aerospace/tech player to demonstrate financial viability.

stories.title: $130 billion in data center projects blocked by protests so far this year
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Significant financial impact: $130 billion in data center projects have faced roadblocks this year.
The conflict highlights growing public resistance to large-scale AI data center development.
The narrative suggests that successful local opposition provides a sense of 'political power' to protestors.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) where lexical/semantic similarity fails for complex reasoning.
It introduces Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT) as a novel post-training framework.
RA-RFT aims to enable language models to learn reasoning by analogy, moving beyond simple knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Mana is a new sim-to-real framework designed to address the complex problem of dexterous manipulation using articulated tools.
The research tackles the difficulty of coordinating internal degrees of freedom and managing contact-rich interactions in robotics.
It specifically addresses the gap in prior work, which often focused on rigid objects rather than complex, articulated tools.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the fundamental challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It focuses on how the design of the 'action interface' used to invoke specialized perception tools limits agent capabilities.
The research investigates the impact of this interface design on an agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of research reproducibility.
This addresses the current resource-intensive and difficult-to-scale manual process in social and behavioral sciences.
The study tested LLM-generated analyses against original findings across 76 published studies.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 addresses a gap in current LLM research agents by focusing on scientific knowledge orchestration, moving beyond simple abstract or citation analysis.
The system converts raw scientific documents into 'agent-native' knowledge, capturing crucial details like entities, claims, evidence, and method lineages.
This work aims to significantly improve scientific reasoning capabilities within AI agents by providing a richer, structured understanding of research papers.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) regarding AI's impact on cognition.
It posits that System 0 offers a unique theoretical lens that existing models cannot fully capture.
The core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' describing AI's influence on human reasoning.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Beyond Runtime Enforcement: Shield Synthesis as Defensibility Analysis for Adversarial Networks
stories.author: Sultan Almuhammadi
bullet_points: The paper re-frames 'Shielded reinforcement learning' from a runtime safety mechanism to a design-time analytical tool.
It proposes using automata-theoretic machinery (like specification compilation and product game construction) for structural analysis of adversarial networks.
The core argument is that the outputs should provide structural insights into the system rather than merely enforcing runtime constraints.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous manipulation using articulated tools in robotics.
Introduces Mana, a general sim-to-real framework designed to tackle this complexity.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies for articulated tools.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models on embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The paper investigates the geometric principles governing knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the traditional view of catastrophic forgetting.
It introduces 'Recovery Subspace Dimensionality' ($k_t$), a novel metric to quantify the complexity of recovering previously learned knowledge.
The study validates its findings using Split CIFAR-100 and a sequentially trained ResNet-18 across ten tasks.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Introduces a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model for predicting spatially varying wildfire spread.
Develops an optimization framework to design effective, targeted aerial suppression strategies.
Addresses operational and environmental uncertainty inherent in real-world wildfire management.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a critical gap in current LLM evaluations, which often assume static operational environments.
Focuses on enabling agents to continually align their knowledge and behavior as environments and tasks evolve.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning tasks.
Conventional retrieval methods (lexical/semantic similarity) fail because similar problems may require different solution strategies.
It introduces Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT) as a new post-training framework to improve reasoning.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The research introduces 'Influcoder,' a method to create an encoder for Data Attribution (DA).
DA methods aim to quantify how specific training data samples influence a model's output, such as identifying sources of toxic behavior in LLMs.
The technique distills gradient influence rankings from decoders into the encoder structure.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents.
Proposes 'HyperTool,' a unified, MCP-style tool interface to improve agent workflows.
Aims to reduce context consumption by abstracting low-level, step-wise tool calls from the main reasoning trace.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method contrasts with existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by checking if the direct answer matches the answer derived from a stated query decomposition.
The research leverages Operad theory, a mathematical formalism for iterated substitution, to provide a new perspective on LLM reliability during inference.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Renault: Electric motors with no rare earths
stories.author: bestouff
bullet_points: Renault is developing electric motors that do not rely on rare earth elements.
This development addresses a major supply chain and environmental concern in the EV industry.
The news signals a potential shift towards more sustainable and resource-independent EV technology.
